title: Australia Data Residency Baseline (Privacy Act 1988 / APPs)
notes: Constrains placement to Australian cloud regions and evidences the Australian Privacy Principles (Schedule 1 of the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth)). APP 8 governs cross-border disclosure and s 16C makes the disclosing entity accountable for an overseas recipient's acts. Evidence of technical/organizational controls, not legal advice or a cryptographic data-path proof.

rules.name: Workloads pinned to an Australian region
rules.description: Every workload declares node placement constrained to an allow-listed Australian cloud region (Sydney/Melbourne/Canberra) via topology.kubernetes.io/region.
rules.rationale: Scheduling workloads outside Australia is a cross-border disclosure of personal information; under APP 8.1 the entity must ensure the overseas recipient does not breach the APPs, and under s 16C remains accountable for the recipient's acts.

rules.name: No unrestricted or cross-border egress
rules.description: Services do not proxy to external endpoints (ExternalName) and NetworkPolicies do not permit unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0, ::/0) egress.
rules.rationale: Open egress or external-name proxying can disclose personal information to an overseas recipient without the reasonable steps APP 8.1 requires.

rules.name: Storage uses customer-managed keys
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims reference a customer-managed key (CMK) so the entity controls the keys.
rules.rationale: Customer-managed keys are a reasonable step to protect personal information from misuse and unauthorised access under APP 11.

rules.name: Encryption at rest declared
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims declare encryption at rest via the configured label/annotation.
rules.rationale: Encryption at rest is a baseline reasonable step to secure personal information under APP 11.
